disclaimer: OCSE preliminary data is self-reported by states and subject to revision. Multi-year trends and cross-metric inconsistencies surface patterns worth investigating but are not dispositive evidence of fraud. Each flag links to the OCSE tables used so anyone can verify the math.

flags.explanation: Actual incentive payments exceeded estimates by >20% in 6 years (avg overrun 47.4%). Persistent overruns of this magnitude go beyond normal forecast variance and suggest the state is either sandbagging its pre-year projections or the performance formula is being revised mid-cycle in ways that favor it.

Non-TANF collections grew 15.9% while TANF/Foster Care collections fell -63.0% across 2017-2024. The program is shifting away from its original mandate (recovering public-assistance outlays) into a general debt-collection operation against private families.
Caseload dropped >10% while collections stayed flat or rose. Possible case-selection bias (retaining high-yield cases) or double-count.

Actual incentive payments exceeded estimates by >20% in 4 years (avg overrun 36.2%). Persistent overruns of this magnitude go beyond normal forecast variance and suggest the state is either sandbagging its pre-year projections or the performance formula is being revised mid-cycle in ways that favor it.

Actual incentive payments exceeded estimates by >20% in 6 years (avg overrun 36.1%). Persistent overruns of this magnitude go beyond normal forecast variance and suggest the state is either sandbagging its pre-year projections or the performance formula is being revised mid-cycle in ways that favor it.
Cost-effectiveness ratio above 10:1 exceeds the national norm (~5-6:1). May indicate non-standard accounting or exclusion of non-IV-D costs.
